glossary.definition: The smallest unit of an element that retains the chemical properties of that element, consisting of a nucleus and electrons.
A pure substance consisting of only one type of atom, characterized by its atomic number.
A substance formed when two or more elements chemically bond together in fixed proportions.
The smallest unit of a compound that retains the chemical properties of that compound, formed by atoms bonded together.
A chemical bond formed between a metal and a nonmetal through the transfer of electrons, resulting in charged ions.
A chemical bond formed between two nonmetals through the sharing of electrons.
The combining capacity of an element, determined by the number of electrons it can gain, lose, or share.
A number assigned to an element in a chemical compound that represents the number of electrons lost or gained.
The ability of an atom to attract electrons in a chemical bond, measured on a scale from 0 to 4.
A substance that donates protons (H+ ions) in solution and has a pH less than 7.
A substance that accepts protons (H+ ions) in solution and has a pH greater than 7.
A measure of the acidity or basicity of a solution, ranging from 0 (most acidic) to 14 (most basic), with 7 being neutral.
A process in which substances are transformed into different substances through the breaking and forming of chemical bonds.
A substance that participates in a chemical reaction and is consumed in the process.
A substance formed as a result of a chemical reaction.
A substance that speeds up a chemical reaction without being consumed or permanently changed.
A state in a reversible reaction where the forward and reverse reactions occur at equal rates.
A chemical reaction that releases energy in the form of heat or light.
A chemical reaction that absorbs energy from its surroundings, usually in the form of heat.
The mass of one mole of a substance, expressed in grams per mole (g/mol).

studyTopics.title: Atomic Structure
studyTopics.description: Learn about the composition of atoms and their subatomic particles

studyTopics.title: Periodic Table
studyTopics.description: Understand the organization and trends of the periodic table

studyTopics.title: Chemical Bonding
studyTopics.description: Explore how atoms bond together to form compounds

studyTopics.title: Chemical Reactions
studyTopics.description: Learn about different types of chemical reactions

quizzes.title: Atomic Structure Basics
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge of atomic structure

questions.question: What is the smallest unit of an element?
questions.explanation: An atom is the smallest unit of an element that retains its chemical properties.

questions.question: What is the center of an atom called?
questions.explanation: The nucleus is the central part of an atom containing protons and neutrons.

quizzes.title: Chemical Bonding
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge of chemical bonds

questions.question: What type of bond forms between a metal and a nonmetal?
questions.explanation: Ionic bonds form between metals and nonmetals through electron transfer.
